title: Web Services Call-In API

Web Services Call-In API

Web Services Call-In API

The Web services call-in API provides a (deprecated) mechanism for implementing enhanced security for Web services. It allows you to execute your own 3GL code prior to Uniface processing the SOAP envelope, and just after Uniface has processed the SOAP request.

Note: The functionality provided by this 3GL API is now available using SOAP callback operations. For new development, use the callback operations rather than the API functions. For more information, see SOAP Callback Operations.

There are a number of different methods proposed for providing extra security for Web services using SOAP. Uniface does not directly support any of these proposals, but does provide a mechanism for users to implement their own enhanced security.

The API provides hooks into the Web services call-in that allow you to execute your own 3GL code prior to Uniface processing the SOAP envelope and just after Uniface has processed the SOAP request. In this way, your 3GL code has access to any information that is contained in the SOAP header. This could be a digital signature or information about the encoding. In addition to the SOAP envelope, information that was gathered in the Web server is also made available.

The Web Services Enhanced Security sample in the samples directory demonstrates the use of this API. The sample library is provided in the bin directory.

API Functions

An implementation must provide one or both of the two main functions soap_pre_request() and soap_post_request(), and can optionally provide the soap_rel_buf() function.

  • soap_pre_request—receives the SOAP envelope just before Uniface would normally process it. It returns either an amended SOAP envelope that Uniface processes, or returns an error message that is encapsulated in a SOAP Fault element and sent as a reply to the Web server.
  • soap_post_request—receives the SOAP envelope containing the reply that Uniface produced. It returns the SOAP envelope to be sent as a reply to the Web server.
  • soap_rel_buf—releases the buffer used for the amended SOAP envelope buffer, or clears the context area if dynamic memory allocation is used.

Specify the entry points in your own 3GL library for the Web services call-in functions, for example:

[USER_3GL]
d:/uniface/3gl/soaphandler (soap_pre_request, soap_post_request, soap_rel_buf)

The API function names are fixed, but you have your choice of name (and location) of the library.

Related Links
  • SOAP Call-In to Uniface From a Web Service Client

    A Uniface service can be deployed as a web service by exporting the service's interface definition into a WSDL file, publishing it on the web site, and deploying the service in the Uniface web service deployment environment. Clients that use the Uniface web service need to send a WSDL request to get the interface definition of this service, and send web service requests to call operations provided in the service.

  • SOAP Faults

    SOAP messages are XML-based, and the SOAP protocol defines a SOAP Fault element for returning remote errors to the caller.
